title: Safe in my own body again
blurb: Healing from trauma at a pace that honoured, not overwhelmed.
lead: A story about the long, gentle work of feeling safe again after something overwhelming.

blocks.text: After it happened, the body stayed on alert. Small sounds triggered huge reactions. Sleep brought nightmares. Sometimes the past felt more present than the present — a flashback dragging everything back.

For a long time, there was shame in it. Why couldn't the mind just move on? But trauma, a therapist explained, lives in the nervous system, not just memory. The reactions weren't weakness — they were a body that had learned to protect itself.

Healing was slow, and that turned out to be the point. Not forcing the memories, but building safety first: grounding when a flashback came, orienting to the present, learning the danger was over. Going at a pace the nervous system could handle.
Safety came back in pieces. A full night's sleep. A loud noise that stayed just a noise. A growing sense that the body was, finally, a place it was okay to be.
Healing wasn't about being brave enough to relive it. It was about being gentle enough to finally feel safe.
This is an illustrative composite — a realistic story drawn from common experiences, not a real named individual. It's here to remind you that change is possible.
